title: Speakers, performers announced for ‘No Kings’ Boston rally Saturday
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey, Rep. Ayanna Pressley, and Dropkick Murphys will appear at the 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common.
The 'No Kings' rally is expected to draw over 100,000 participants, from 2 to 4 p.m. on March 28.
Speakers will address issues like LGBTQIA+ rights and U.S. aggression; activities include a mural and ice sculpture display.

title: Report: Celtics executive Brad Stevens removes his name from UNC coaching search
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics executive Brad Stevens has removed his name from consideration for the UNC basketball coaching position.
Stevens, a former Butler coach, has been successful in his role as Celtics president, winning NBA executive of the year in 2024.
UNC, which recently fired Hubert Davis, had Stevens at the top of its candidate list for head coach.

title: Free Fenway Franks? Diner rolls out Opening Day deal for Red Sox fans.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: South Street Diner in Boston offers free Fenway Franks with beverage purchase on April 3 from 11 a.m. to 9 p.m.
The offer coincides with the Red Sox home opener against the San Diego Padres, with game shown on the diner's TVs.
Uber Eats orders over $20 between 2 p.m. and 6 p.m. include a free Fenway Frank for fans celebrating at home.

title: MLB analyst identified the biggest unknown facing the 2026 Red Sox
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Red Sox's primary 2026 question is whether their current roster can provide sufficient offensive power.
MLB reporter Ian Browne highlights Roman Anthony, Wilyer Abreu, and Ceddanne Rafaela as potential key sluggers.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts Wilyer Abreu could surpass 30 home runs this season.

title: Boston Marathon runner honors brother lost to suicide, supports fellow veterans
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Heather Diamond will run the Boston Marathon to honor her brother, Scott, who was a US Navy veteran.
Diamond is raising funds for Home Base, supporting veterans with war-related mental health issues.
Home Base offers free treatment to veterans, a collaboration between Massachusetts General Hospital and The Red Sox Foundation.

title: Livestream: Day 3 of the Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Prosecutors rested their case after calling their last witness on Wednesday morning in Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons opted for a bench trial, having a judge decide her fate instead of a jury.
Testimonies include the officer who shot Kelsey Fitzsimmons, continuing from day 2 of the trial.

title: Police release photo of ‘person of interest’ in Boston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police released a surveillance photo of a person of interest in a South End shooting incident.
The suspect allegedly discarded clothing before fleeing and disguising as a construction worker, police reported.
Investigators urge the public to submit tips via phone, text, or the Boston Police Crime Stoppers website.

title: Why did Kelsey Fitzsimmons choose to have a judge decide her fate?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons is opting for a bench trial, facing assault charges after allegedly pointing a gun at a fellow officer.
Fitzsimmons's defense team selected a judge trial to avoid jurors' emotional reactions and focus on factual evidence.
Fitzsimmons's postpartum depression is a key element; experts suggest a judge might assess the mental health defense more fairly.

title: The road is only going to get harder for Bruins after ’embarrassing’ loss to Leafs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins lost 4-2 to the Toronto Maple Leafs, failing to secure crucial points in the playoff race.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney had expressed confidence in the team before the loss, citing their playoff potential.
Boston sits in the first wild-card spot with 86 points, closely followed by Ottawa and New York with 85 points.

title: Adam Schefter: No ‘under-the-table’ A.J. Brown trade in place for Patriots, Eagles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Adam Schefter refutes rumors of Patriots having a secret deal for Eagles' A.J. Brown trade.
Eagles face $43.5 million dead cap if they trade A.J. Brown before June 1, reducing after.
Patriots could pursue other wide receivers or draft options before any potential A.J. Brown deal.
